source: FFIEC BSA/AML Manual Appendix G - Structuring
note: Structuring transactions to evade BSA reporting and recordkeeping requirements can result in civil and criminal penalties under 31 USC 5324. The $10,000 CTR threshold is the primary reference point. Two transactions slightly under threshold conducted days or weeks apart do not necessarily constitute structuring — further review is required.
definition: A person structures a transaction if that person, acting alone or in conjunction with or on behalf of other persons, conducts or attempts to conduct one or more transactions in currency in any amount at one or more financial institutions on one or more days in any manner for the purpose of evading CTR filing requirements. Breaking down a single currency sum exceeding $10,000 into smaller amounts is the most common form, but transactions need not exceed $10,000 at any one bank on any single day to constitute structuring.

indicators.text: Customer structures currency deposit or withdrawal transactions so that each is less than the $10,000 CTR filing threshold.

Customer uses currency to purchase official bank checks money orders or traveler's checks in amounts less than $10,000 and possibly in amounts less than the $3,000 recordkeeping threshold to avoid producing identification.
Customer exchanges small bank notes for large ones in amounts less than $10,000.
Currency deposits or withdrawals conducted just below the $10,000 CTR reporting threshold on multiple occasions.

Deposits of monetary instruments that may have been purchased elsewhere are structured to evade CTR filing requirements or recordkeeping requirements for the currency purchase of monetary instruments.
Monetary instruments are numbered sequentially in groups totaling less than $10,000 or $3,000.
Monetary instruments bear the same handwriting and often the same small mark stamp or initials suggesting a common origin.
Monetary instruments appear to have been purchased at numerous places on the same or different days.
Structuring may occur before a customer brings funds to a bank — the bank may only be able to identify the aftermath of structuring through patterns in deposited monetary instruments.
No CTRs filed despite significant currency transaction activity suggesting transactions were deliberately kept below reporting thresholds.
